rationale.root_cause: In CWE416_Use_After_Free__malloc_free_char_01_bad, data is freed (around line 34) and then immediately used via printLine(data) (around line 36), causing a use-after-free on the dangling pointer. The defect is the dereference/use of data after it has been freed.
rationale.local_fix: Move the use (printLine(data)) before free(data), or keep data allocated until after printing. This removes the use of the buffer after it has been freed.
rationale.global_fix: Adopt explicit ownership/RAII: in C++ use smart pointers (e.g., std::unique_ptr<char[]>) to manage lifetime automatically. In C, establish clear ownership conventions (single owner frees, no use after transfer) to make lifetime explicit.
